_meta.purpose: Frozen config for XGBoost SMOTE+tuned baseline — source of truth for Experiments 3 (ADASYN) and 4 (Focal Loss)
_meta.ambiguity_note: Two SMOTE+tuned runs exist: (A) pure XGBoost on 14 feats (F1=0.8646 per verified_metrics.json 'XGBoost SMOTE+tuned'), and (B) AE+XGBoost on 15 feats incl. recon_error (F1=0.869 per 'AE + XGBoost SMOTE+tuned'). Task spec says F1=0.8646 → use config A (14 feats, no recon_error) for Exp 3+4.

fixed.scale_pos_weight: NOT USED — SMOTE balances classes instead (for Exp 4 focal loss, also NOT USED)

xgboost_hyperparameters.best_params_persistence: NOT hardcoded — only printed at runtime. Saved .joblib file (xgboost_smote_tuned.joblib) contains the fitted model; extract best_params from there via joblib.load().get_params()

smote_config_for_reference.applied_to: X_train only, AFTER train/test split (pre-split CSVs are used directly). Test set NEVER receives SMOTE.

feature_selection_logic: drop_cols = ['is_fraud', 'unix_time']; feature_cols = all other columns of fraudTrain_engineered.csv after dropping those.

train_test_split.method: Pre-split files used directly — fraudTrain_engineered.csv as train, fraudTest_engineered.csv as test

preprocessing.scaling: NONE — XGBoost on raw (unscaled) features. StandardScaler was only applied to AE input in AE+XGBoost path.
preprocessing.feature_order: Use column order from feature_cols list above

experiment_3_adasyn_plan.xgboost_reuse: Load xgboost_smote_tuned.joblib to extract tuned hyperparameters; refit a fresh XGBClassifier with those params on ADASYN-resampled train
fallback_chain: If ADASYN fails: retry with n_neighbors=3
If still fails: retry with sampling_strategy=0.5
If still fails: log failure honestly in verified_metrics.json, move on

experiment_4_focal_loss_plan.objective: custom focal loss via XGBoost's `obj` parameter
experiment_4_focal_loss_plan.formula: FL(p) = -(1-p)^gamma * log(p) for positives, -(p)^gamma * log(1-p) for negatives
experiment_4_focal_loss_plan.resampling: NONE — focal loss handles imbalance internally
experiment_4_focal_loss_plan.class_weight: NONE — focal loss + alpha handles it
experiment_4_focal_loss_plan.xgboost_reuse: Same tuned hyperparameters minus objective; set custom obj function
experiment_4_focal_loss_plan.fallback: If custom gradient/hessian fails: use imblearn.ensemble.BalancedBaggingClassifier with XGBClassifier base estimator. Label result 'XGBoost_BalancedBagging (Focal Loss unavailable)'.

red_flags: Data leakage via SMOTE inside RandomizedSearchCV CV folds (moderate): X_train_smote is created ONCE before search.fit(), so each CV validation fold sees SMOTE-synthesized samples derived from its own training portion. Inflates CV F1 but does NOT contaminate test (SMOTE not applied to test). Fix would require imblearn Pipeline. For Exp 3 same issue will apply unless fixed — recommend using tuned hyperparameters from .joblib and skipping re-tuning (re-tuning would be a different experiment anyway).
Autoencoder is non-deterministic (no torch.manual_seed) in the 15-feature AE+XGBoost path. Not relevant for Exp 3/4 since those use 14-feature (no AE) path.
Best hyperparameters only printed, not saved as code. Must load from xgboost_smote_tuned.joblib (.get_params()) to reuse.
No explicit tree_method set — relies on XGBoost 3.2.0 defaults (hist on CPU). Should be set explicitly for reproducibility.
reg_alpha / reg_lambda not tuned — defaults used. Not a bug but note.
Paths are relative (good, works locally without edits).
Test set never contaminated. Clean separation.
XGBoost 3.2.0 behavior may differ from whatever version was used to produce F1=0.8646 — version drift risk. Check xgboost version compatibility of xgboost_smote_tuned.joblib.
blockers_to_deterministic_rerun: Extract best_params from xgboost_smote_tuned.joblib before running Exp 3/4
Verify xgboost 3.2.0 loads the .joblib (version mismatch possible)
For GPU speedup: set tree_method='hist' + device='cuda' (xgboost 2+ syntax) IF a CUDA-aware xgboost install is available

baseline_cw_config_for_reference.note: This is the 'class weighting' row in Gap 2 table (F1=0.656 at thr 0.7)
